channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Ben Talty placed on report for hip drop tackle on James Tedesco
item.description: During a recent NRL clash, Ben Talty has come under scrutiny after performing a hip drop tackle on Sydney Roosters star James Tedesco. The incident occurred in the midst of a high-intensity match and ...

item.title: Moshi teachers advised to avoid pension fraud and plan retirement early
item.description: Teachers in Moshi Rural District, Kilimanjaro Region, who are preparing to retire have been strongly advised to stay alert against rising pension fraud cases. The warning was given during a special tr...

item.title: Benue State Head of Service, Dr. Agbogbo Ode, Dies After Brief Illness
item.description: The Benue State government don announce say the Head of Service, Dr. Agbogbo Ode, don die. He waka comot dis world on Saturday morning, May 2, 2026, after small sickness wey no too last. The Secretary...

item.title: Two teenagers hospitalised after violent brawl in Southend
item.description: A violent confrontation in Southend has resulted in two teenagers being admitted to hospital with knife injuries. Authorities reported that the altercation involved a large group of individuals, escal...

item.title: Britney Spears Faces DUI Charges After March Arrest in California
item.description: Pop star Britney Spears has been formally charged with driving under the influence following an arrest by California police in March. According to reports, Spears was stopped by law enforcement for al...

item.title: Trump Declines Iran’s Offer for Diplomatic Talks Amid Ongoing Conflict
item.description: US President Donald Trump has dismissed the latest Iranian proposal to engage in talks aimed at ending the ongoing Middle East conflict. Despite a ceasefire that has held since 8 April, tensions remai...

item.title: TMC files complaint with EC over alleged unauthorised handling of postal ballot covers ahead of Bengal vote count
item.description: The Trinamool Congress (TMC) in West Bengal has filed a complaint with the Election Commission, accusing officials of unauthorised sorting of postal ballot covers at a strongroom in Kolkata, where Ele...

item.title: FCC Rules Against Lifetime Benefits for Retired Balochistan Chief Secretaries and Their Widows
item.description: In a significant ruling, the Federal Constitutional Court (FCC) of Pakistan rejected a plea by the Balochistan government that sought to extend lifetime facilities to retired chief secretaries and the...

item.title: Trump Criticizes US Navy, Germany Prepares for Troop Withdrawal, and Tensions Rise Between US and Iran
item.description: The situation in the Middle East is rapidly evolving. Former President Donald Trump made waves by calling the US Navy's actions during the seizure of an Iranian ship 'pirate-like', drawing mixed react...

item.title: Treasury Plans to Halve Road Levy Allocation to Reduce Fuel Costs
item.description: The Kenyan government, through the National Treasury, is proposing changes to the Road Maintenance Levy Fund Act aimed at lowering fuel prices for wananchi. The proposal seeks to reduce the portion of...

item.title: Former Friend of Sarah Ferguson Condemns Her Ties to Jeffrey Epstein
item.description: Sarah Ferguson, Duchess of York, has faced renewed scrutiny over her association with convicted paedophile Jeffrey Epstein. Tonia Sargerson, a 64-year-old mother of five from a Hull council estate, on...

item.title: Funguo Program Supports Tanzanian Student Entrepreneurs with New Funding and Recognition
item.description: The Funguo Innovation Programme, under the United Nations Development Programme (UNDP), recently concluded its #YouthIgnite Third Cohort Award Ceremony and launched the 5th FUNGUO Catalytic Funding Ca...

item.title: Louisiana Faces Election Disruption After House Primary Delay
item.description: Louisiana has been thrown into confusion after state officials delayed the U.S. House primary while other races remain on schedule. The move has left voters, local election administrators, candidates,...

item.title: Tanzania Adds Seven Boats to Improve Vaccination Access in Coastal Areas
item.description: The Tanzanian government has boosted its vaccination services by launching seven boats worth 1.1 billion shillings to reach citizens in the Coast Region. This initiative, announced during National Hea...

item.title: Calgary man faces first-degree murder charges after deaths of his two young children
item.description: A tragic incident unfolded in northwest Calgary when two young children, a five-year-old boy and a three-year-old girl, were found dead in a vehicle on Thursday. Their father, aged 37, has been charge...

item.title: Alto CEO Confirms No Downtown Ottawa Stop Planned
item.description: Alto, the Toronto-based ride-hailing service, has confirmed that it will not establish a downtown Ottawa stop, citing concerns over potential negative impacts on the surrounding neighbourhood. The com...

item.title: Mary Jane Veloso’s Fight Highlights the Plight of Overseas Filipino Workers
item.description: Mary Jane Veloso’s story is often described as miraculous, yet it reflects the harsh realities faced by many overseas Filipino workers (OFWs). Veloso, who left the Philippines in 2009 to work abroad d...

item.title: Karachi experiences extreme heat with temperatures reaching 45°C
item.description: KARACHI: The port city of Karachi continued to face intense heat and humidity on Thursday, with the official maximum temperature recorded at 36.1°C, while the ‘feels like’ temperature soared to 45°C. ...

item.title: Archbishop Muheria Calls for Honest Leadership Amid Economic Challenges
item.description: Nyeri Metropolitan Archbishop Anthony Muheria has once again criticised President William Ruto and the Kenya Kwanza administration over what he describes as unfulfilled promises to Kenyans. Speaking d...

item.title: Two Killed and Nineteen Injured in Head-On Collision on N2 Near Mthatha
item.description: A tragic road accident occurred on the N2 near Sibangweni Village outside Mthatha, where a minibus taxi and a bakkie collided head-on, resulting in two fatalities and injuring nineteen others, some cr...
